comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout as an architectural issue, not a compile/runtime break. extensions/tau/src/shared/effect-tool.ts defines decodeParams as a synchronous function (line 21) and execute as returning Promise<AgentToolResult<TDetails>> | AgentToolResult<TDetails> (lines 31-34). defineEffectTool(...) then just try/catches those calls and returns a ToolDefinition (lines 63-80). It does not accept an Effect program, a typed Effect runner, or preserve Effect error/environment channels across the helper boundary. So the 'still Promise-first' part is accurate. The 'wrong abstraction name' part is subjective, but the current helper does not enforce Effect-native tool composition.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Implemented the rename path (rather than forcing a fake Effect abstraction). Replaced shared helper `defineEffectTool`/`effect-tool.ts` with neutral `defineDecodedTool` in new file `extensions/tau/src/shared/decoded-tool.ts`; renamed associated context/options types accordingly; removed `extensions/tau/src/shared/effect-tool.ts`; and updated all call sites/imports (agent/tool.ts, exa/index.ts, memory/index.ts, skill-manage/index.ts, thread/index.ts). Behavior is unchanged: helper still decodes params + wraps execute try/catch, but naming now matches what it actually does.
